what_it_does: This file is the root React component for the Virtual Hub viewer. It maintains state for the active hub, a quick-menu toggle, the currently open panel (info/share/floorplan), and a click-to-place capture comment. It renders a full-screen photo stage with a vignette, a hub switcher, a menu button, conditional panels/overlays, and an onboarding hint when nothing is open.
observations: Hardcoded HUBS array drives hub labels and titles.
Default active hub is 'apt-a' and default panel is 'info'.
selectPanel closes the menu only for the 'info', 'share', and 'floorplan' ids.
handleStageClick restricts capture to clicks whose target has class 'vh-stage' or 'vh-vignette'.
FloorplanOverlay receives activeHub 'living' when the active hub is 'apt-a', otherwise it receives the active hub id.
An inline-styled onboarding hint appears only when menuOpen, panel, and capture are all falsy.
The component is mounted with ReactDOM.createRoot on #app at the bottom of the file.
standouts: Hardcoded mapping of 'apt-a' to floorplan id 'living'.
Inline styled onboarding hint rather than a separate component or token-driven style.
conventions: Conditional rendering with && and ternary expressions.
Class-name guard to distinguish background clicks from overlay clicks.
State setter aliases via destructured rename (useState_vh).
concerns: Class-list check on e.target may be brittle if the stage contains elements without the expected classes.
Floorplan 'living' mapping is hardcoded and not explained in this file.
ReactDOM.createRoot call assumes #app exists and is not guarded.
Inline onboarding styles bypass the design-system tokens.
notes: This is the top-level orchestrator for the Virtual Hub viewer, wiring hub switching, quick-menu panels, floorplan overlay, and click-to-comment capture.
questions: Where are HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, and CaptureComment defined?
Is 'living' a stable floorplan identifier or a temporary mapping?
Should the onboarding hint auto-dismiss after first interaction?
What happens if the stage contains child elements that do not carry 'vh-stage' or 'vh-vignette' classes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose 'App.jsx — Virtual Hub viewer root' matches the actual content.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a single full-screen stage where a user can browse hubs, open lightweight info/share/floorplan panels, and drop contextual comments directly on the photo background.
novelty: The capture-comment interaction that records exact click coordinates on the stage background and immediately suppresses other UI state.
contribution: It contributes the root state model and interaction rules that coordinate hub selection, menu/panel visibility, floorplan overlay activation, and background click-to-comment behavior for the Virtual Hub viewer.
summary_for_embedding: HubApp.jsx is the root React component for the Virtual Hub viewer, managing hub selection, quick-menu panels, floorplan overlay, and click-to-comment capture on a full-screen photo stage.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the main container wiring together navigation, informational panels, and contextual annotation for an immersive hub viewer.

symbols.does: Root React component that orchestrates the Virtual Hub viewer state and layout.
symbols.description: HubApp holds state for the active hub, menu visibility, active panel, and capture-comment coordinates. It renders the full-screen stage, hub switcher, menu button, conditional panels and overlays, and an onboarding hint when no overlay is open.

symbols.does: Updates the active panel and closes the quick menu for info, share, or floorplan selections.
symbols.description: selectPanel sets the panel state to the chosen id and dismisses the menu for the three known panel types, centralizing the transition from menu selection to panel display.

symbols.does: Records a click position on the stage background and opens a capture comment.
symbols.description: handleStageClick checks that the click target is the stage or vignette element, computes coordinates relative to the stage bounds, stores them as the capture state, and closes the menu so a comment marker can be placed without UI interference.
